inline_comments.message: where is the certificate coming from?
oh... please move this to a separate file
please use our service account template (you can find it in common/common)
do you really need your own PG instance? How about using the shared one?

Currently, all certs are put into the docker images during the building phase. If there's a better way to manage them, please let me know and give me some guidance. Actually, I've looked into the codes of MSB and it seems that the certs are also managed by Helm, but I don't know how to complete the configuration.
Thanks in advance.
OK. Expected to be done today.

For the Honolulu release, I suggest we use a separate instance for Holmes. This is to make the change "backward-compatible". At least at the moment, we have to take the contingency that we may not finish the migration in R8 in to consideration, then we may fall back to the Cloudify-based deployment without any changes in our code. After the migration is completed, we may optimize Holmes to use the shared PG instance.

The preferred way to handle certificates is to use certInitializer just grep for it and you will find dozen of examples. You will also need to update AAF database just like in this review:
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/116385

Complete blueprint is not required here; only application_config parameters are needed. These should be converted to json file for consul-loader-container:1.0.0 to load them into consul.

Alternatively, if you have cloudify deployment of holmes in your env - you can copy the json config from consul directly to here.

shoudl reflect the key underwhich holmes configuration should be placed

Based on current deployments via cloudify, the consul key should be  "holmes-engine-mgmt" and file should be json representation of corresponding application config to be created under resources for uploading into consul

Complete blueprint should not be included here.. only the application_config (line #94-100) is needed (basically the config which holmes expects when doing CBS fetch). 

As the current version of consul-loader-container:1.0.0 supports only json - the required application config should be converted to json

As noted earlier, you can copy the json config loaded into consul from current/cloudify based deployments directly
again this key should map to key holmes will be querrying CBS. This should likely be "holmes-rule-mgmt" and file should map json representation of application config created under resources
please add here postgres as we discussed for now we are going to use a dedicated instance for now

Add here also root password. Take a look at vnfsdk/values.yaml and copy-paste most of their postgres section

which modules access PG DB?
replace with actual value required (get_input is cloudify function)

I'm a newbie to Helm, so I just copied the configuration from the DCAE sub-components. Now the image is not global.
They are currently hard-coded within the containers. We're now optimizing that...

This is because the rules (during the onbording phase) of Holmes were designed so from the very beginning. For now, we have no idea how to optimize it. Needs more time to discuss it with both DCAE and CLAMP.
please put at end of L29 with "nindent" instead of "indent"

hardcoded is container is no go since francfurt.
please use certInitializer.

* the default user id

well I saw you started to tackle it, good

here you need to set: 
      port: 9102
here you need to set: port: &svc_port 9102

you should use helm templating for that:
"ControlLoop-VOLTE-2179b738-fd36-4843-a71a-a8c24c70c55b$$$package org.onap.holmes.droolsRule;\n\n {{ "resources/python/holmes.droolsRules.py"Get . }}"
you have one empty line too much, please remove L22 or L23
put at the end of L29 and use "nindent" instead of "indent"
please remove surnumerous empty line (L21 or L22)
in order to use yaml anchor, service part should be before liveness and readiness parts
you should not hardcode namespace name here and below
